summary: 6 newsletters processed. 4 critical/high AI relevance, 1 low relevance (tech worker survey), 0 irrelevant. Key topics: Claude Opus 4.8 (Anthropic), AI2 organizational changes, GitHub agents strategy, Microsoft MAI model family announcement, BMW humanoid robotics deployment.

newsletters.subject: Opus 4.8
newsletters.rationale: Anthropic Claude Opus 4.8 release — major model update from a leading frontier lab. Directly relevant to LLM entities and model comparison pages. Ben's Bites is a primary AI news source.
newsletters.notes: All 20 links are opaque Substack redirect URLs. The primary article URL is the app-link post (publication_id=4379299, post_id=200251376). Links 8-20 are generic Substack redirect tracking links — likely embedded article references within the newsletter body. Requires web scraping to resolve actual article URLs and content.

newsletters.subject: Farewell Ai2
newsletters.rationale: Nathan Lambert farewell post about AI2 (Allen Institute for AI) — significant organizational development in open-source AI research. Lambert is a key opinion leader in AI safety/alignment. Could affect existing AI2 entity page and spawn event page.
newsletters.notes: Source is open.substack.com/pub/robotic/p/farewell-ai2. Links 11-20 are generic Substack tracking redirects (likely article references). Link 4 is @natolambert profile. Publication is Nathan Lambert's 'Robotic' newsletter on AI research and open-source AI.

newsletters.subject: Announcing: The second annual tech worker sentiment survey
newsletters.rationale: Lenny's Newsletter (substack.com/@lenny) with co-author Noam Segal (substack.com/@noamsegal). Tech worker sentiment survey — tangential to AI industry but not directly about LLM/AI technology. Only 6 links, mostly tracking redirects.
newsletters.notes: Not AI/LLM specific. Could be relevant for AI industry labor trends if part of a broader concept page, but not priority for wiki ingestion.

newsletters.subject: 🤖 BMW Sends Humanoids to Work
newsletters.rationale: BMW deploying humanoid robots in manufacturing — significant real-world AI deployment. Relates to embodied AI and robotics concepts. All 20 links are Beehiiv tracking redirects — cannot determine actual content without resolution.
newsletters.notes: All links are Beehiiv encrypted tracking URLs (link.mail.beehiiv.com). The source newsletter publisher is unknown — cannot determine from tracking URLs alone. Requires resolving the first link to identify the actual article and publisher.

newsletters.subject: GitHub's plan for Agents — Kyle Daigle, GitHub
newsletters.rationale: GitHub's agent strategy interview with Kyle Daigle (GitHub CTO/CPO-level) from swyx's newsletter. Directly core to wiki focus: AI coding agents, developer tooling, GitHub Copilot ecosystem. Source URL: open.substack.com/pub/swyx/p/github. Publication is swyx (Shawn Wang), a key AI engineering thought leader.
newsletters.notes: swyx (publication_id=1084089) is both 'The AI Engineer Newsletter' and 'AINews'. Links 2-9 are all podcast audio player URLs for the same post (post_id=200249307) — this is a podcast episode with show notes. Links 16-20 are article reference redirects within the newsletter body.

newsletters.subject: [AINews] Microsoft Build: MAI-Thinking-1 and MAI Family models
newsletters.rationale: Microsoft Build conference announcement of MAI-Thinking-1 and MAI model family — major new entrant in frontier LLM space. Directly impacts model entities, comparison pages, and Microsoft/OpenAI ecosystem pages. Fresh (2026-06-03) and high signal from AINews.
newsletters.notes: Source URL: open.substack.com/pub/swyx/p/ainews-microsoft-build-mai-thinking. Links 8-20 are all generic Substack redirect tracking links — likely the actual article links embedded in the newsletter body referencing Microsoft Build coverage, MAI model details, benchmark results, etc.

ingestion_queue.reason: Breaking news (today), new frontier model family from Microsoft. Create entity page + event page.

blockers: All 6 newsletters contain only opaque redirect URLs (Substack app-link/redirect and Beehiiv encrypted tracking). Cannot determine actual article URLs or scrape content without resolving these redirects first.
The triage-next step (newsletter-wiki-ingest) will need to resolve the primary article URL for each high-priority newsletter before wiki pages can be created/updated.
